derivation: SMTP (send/relay), POP3 (download+delete) and IMAP (server-side sync) are all mail protocols, so 'All the above'.
Compiled and ran: x=0 cannot satisfy x<=1 AND x>=10 simultaneously, condition is false on first test, iterations=0, so the loop never executes.

myAnswer: Mealy: present state + present input; Moore: present state only
derivation: Standard FSM definition: Mealy output = f(current state, current inputs); Moore output = f(current state) only.
shippedAnswer:
- Mealy FSM depends on present state and present input
- Moore FSM depends on present state only

derivation: Read the PNG: inputs A,B with Q(n+1) = Q (00, hold), 0 (01, reset), 1 (10, set), Q' (11, toggle). Toggle on 11 is the JK signature (SR would call 11 invalid, D/T have one input).

BST ops walk one root-to-leaf path, cost proportional to height; best/balanced height is log2(n) so O(log n). Minor stem ambiguity: a strict 'best case' for search could be O(1) (key at root), but the intended balanced-tree reading gives O(log n).
The CPU emits logical/virtual addresses; the MMU translates them to physical addresses seen by memory.
Blocked -> Running is impossible: an unblocked process goes Blocked -> Ready, then only the dispatcher moves Ready -> Running. B, C, D are all standard transitions.
SQL is the query/definition language of relational DBMSs; HTML and CSS are presentation/markup, XML is data interchange.
Solaris, Linux and Windows are operating systems; 'Oracle' names an RDBMS/vendor. (Oracle also ships Solaris/Oracle Linux, but the bare name is the database.)
Growth order lg n < n < n lg n < n^2, so the slowest-growing (best) is O(lg n).
Big-O drops constant factors (2000) and lower-order terms (-1): O(2000n - 1) = O(n).
Tree, heap and list organise data; bubble sort is an algorithm operating on data, not a data structure.
Inner loop runs i times for each i = 1..n, so total = sum_{i=1..n} i = n(n+1)/2. Verified by execution: n=8 gives 36 = 8*9/2.
Two recursive calls on n-1 and n-2 plus O(1) non-recursive work (one comparison, one addition): T(n) = T(n-2) + T(n-1) + 1. Option D omits the constant work; +n would require O(n) work in the body.
